extraction.one_line_summary: BERNAL Egan attacked solo halfway up Passo Giau (10km at 10%), extended his GC lead by 27 seconds over BARDET and CARUSO.
tactical_patterns: EF Education Nippo paced early despite CARTHY trailing GC leaders, inverted domestique logic suggests desperation or bluffing rivals.
BERNAL attacked halfway up 10km climb, not at base or summit, neutralizing counterattacks before descent advantage could materialize.
YATES lost 2:36 same day he dropped from 2nd to 5th; mechanical issues cascading into psychological collapse pattern.
Stage abbreviation from 153km to ~93km disproportionately hurt diesel engines like VLASOV, benefiting explosive punchers who thrive without attrition.

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: second stage win with controlled solo move, extending GC lead in brutal conditions shows peak form trajectory
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: jumped to second overall limiting losses to 27 seconds, unexpected resilience suggests podium threat not domestique resignation
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: resurgent ride matching CARUSO at 27 seconds, Form timing suggests third-week strength building after conservative start
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: collapsed from 2nd to 5th losing 2:36, gap widening from mechanical suggests mental fragility under pressure
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: team paced early but finished with CICCONE group, ambition without execution reveals overestimation of current form
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: dropped on climb after mechanical, fell to fourth overall, mechanical resilience failure pattern emerging
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: lost 24 minutes indicating complete abandonment of GC or catastrophic form collapse on shortened mountain stage
